summary: Tracks the escalation from Huawei’s 2019 Entity List designation through temporary carve-outs, foreign-produced direct product (FDP) tightening, FCC market-access restrictions, and a major BIS enforcement action.
Captures how the SMIC case moved from a firm-specific Entity List designation to broader sector-wide semiconductor manufacturing controls affecting PRC fabs and associated supply chains.

case_name: Advanced Computing and Semiconductor Controls Chain
summary: Captures the October 2022–December 2024 tightening cycle spanning advanced-computing controls, UVL-to-Entity-List escalation, semiconductor-manufacturing controls, procurement restrictions, and the 140-entity expansion.

case_name: YMTC Escalation and Memory-Fab Risk Chain
summary: Tracks the shift from YMTC’s October 2022 UVL status to its December 2022 Entity List designation, then follows later memory/HBM-focused controls and downstream procurement-exclusion risk.

Tracks how Section 5949 semiconductor security concerns moved from advance rulemaking in 2024 to a 2026 proposed FAR prohibition naming SMIC, CXMT, and YMTC and adding disclosure/reporting obligations.

case_name: Foundry Diversion and AI-Diffusion Compliance Chain
summary: Tracks how December 2024 advanced-node/HBM/FDP refinements evolved into January 2025 AI-diffusion controls, foundry-specific due-diligence requirements, and a foundry-focused Entity List action tied to diversion risk to Huawei, followed by a February 2025 correction and a January 2026 licensing recalibration.

case_name: CXMT Memory Security and Procurement Chain
summary: Tracks how memory-specific controls and security screening signals evolved into explicit procurement and disclosure risk for CXMT-linked semiconductor sources, connecting HBM-focused export controls, a DoD Chinese military company notice, AI-diffusion framing, and the February 2026 proposed FAR prohibition.

Tracks the shift from a facilitative Data Center VEU framework toward tighter AI-diffusion controls, foundry due-diligence requirements, revocation of PRC fab VEU authorizations, and a later recalibration of chip license review policy.

Tracks the 2025 progression from January Entity List additions to broader March expansion, September anti-diversion enforcement linked to SMIC-listed fabs, and October China/Turkey/UAE additions that widened the circumvention perimeter.

case_name: Semiconductor Import Security and Domestic Capacity Chain
summary: Tracks how AI-infrastructure industrial policy evolved into a January 2026 section 232 semiconductor security finding and a negotiation-based import-adjustment plan, then connected to procurement-side prohibitions and subcontractor flow-down duties.
geo_scope: United States / global semiconductor supply chain
